notes.content: Prompt engineering is the practice of designing prompts that guide an LLM to produce the desired output. It includes role definition, examples, and constraints.
Grounding provides the model with external or structured context it could not know from its training data, such as tool results, documents, or a database.
Agentic behavior means the model decides whether to call a tool, reads the result, and chooses what to do next instead of the application hardcoding the flow.
An MCP tool is defined with a name, description, and input schema, and the model can call it during execution. The application executes the tool and returns results to the model.
Evaluation should include test cases, actual outputs, and a description of what success looks like, including both passes and failures.
